skill.name: file-organizer
skill.description: Intelligently organizes files and folders by understanding context, finding duplicates, and suggesting better organizational structures. Use when user wants to clean up directories, organize downlo...
skill.summary: Intelligently organizes files and folders by understanding context, finding duplicates, and suggesti...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' are example bash commands in markdown code blocks (not actual code execution). The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are YAML frontmatter and date format strings. The 'system reconnaissance' flags are use case descriptions. This is a legitimate file organization skill with no malicious intent.

content.value_statement: This skill helps you clean up cluttered directories by analyzing your files, finding duplicates, and proposing logical folder structures. It automates file organization while asking for confirmation before making any changes.
actual_capabilities: Analyze directory structure and identify file types
Find duplicate files by content hash or name
Suggest logical folder organization based on content
Execute file moves and renames with user approval
Identify old or unused files for archiving
Provide maintenance tips for keeping files organized
limitations: Cannot access files outside specified target directories
Requires user confirmation before deleting or moving files
Does not handle encrypted or password-protected files
May not recognize context-specific organization needs without user input

use_cases.title: Clean Up Downloads Folder
use_cases.description: Organize a cluttered Downloads folder by sorting files into categories like Documents, Images, Archives, and moving old files to archive.
use_cases.target_user: General users with messy download folders

use_cases.title: Project Structure Setup
use_cases.description: Create a logical folder structure for a new project by analyzing the initial files and proposing an organized layout.

use_cases.title: Duplicate File Removal
use_cases.description: Find and remove duplicate files taking up storage space, keeping the newest or best-named version.
use_cases.target_user: Users with limited storage or duplicate file issues

prompt_templates.title: Basic Organization Request
prompt_templates.prompt: My Downloads folder is a mess. Can you help me organize it?
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a general cleanup of their Downloads folder

prompt_templates.title: Find Duplicates
prompt_templates.prompt: Find and remove duplicate files in my Documents folder.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to free up space by removing duplicate files

prompt_templates.title: New Project Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: I have some files for a new coding project. Can you suggest a good folder structure?
prompt_templates.scenario: User is starting a new project and needs organizational advice

prompt_templates.title: Archive Old Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me identify files I have not touched in over 6 months and suggest what to archive.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to clean up by archiving old, unused files

output_examples.output:
I'd be happy to help organize your Downloads folder! Let me first understand the scope:

1. Which directory needs organization? (I'll assume ~/Downloads)
2. What's the main problem? (Can't find things, duplicates, too messy, no structure?)
3. Any files or folders to avoid?
4. How aggressively to organize?

Please let me know your preferences, or say 'yes' to proceed with a standard organization.

I'll scan your Documents folder for duplicate files using content hashing.

Found 3 sets of duplicates:

1. "report_final.pdf" (2 copies, 2.3MB each)
   - ~/Documents/Work/report_final.pdf (modified: 2024-01-15)
   - ~/Documents/Archive/report_final.pdf (modified: 2023-11-20)
   - Recommendation: Keep the newer version in Work/

2. "meeting-notes.docx" (3 copies)
   - Multiple versions found
   - Recommendation: Keep the most recent

Shall I proceed with removing the duplicates?

best_practices: Always confirm with the user before moving or deleting any files
Start with analyzing the current structure before proposing changes
Group files by type, purpose, or date based on user preferences
anti_patterns: Deleting files without asking for confirmation first
Moving files to unknown locations without logging the changes
Organizing files the user explicitly asked to skip or avoid

faq.question: Will this skill delete my files?
faq.answer: No. This skill will never delete files without your explicit confirmation. It will always ask before removing anything.

faq.question: Can it organize my entire computer?
faq.answer: It can help organize any directory you specify, but you should start with a single folder like Downloads or Documents to keep things manageable.

faq.question: How does it find duplicates?
faq.answer: It uses file content hashing (MD5) to find exact duplicates, and can also identify files with similar names or sizes.

faq.question: What if I do not like the organization plan?
faq.answer: You can modify the proposed plan or decline entirely. The skill will ask for your approval before making any changes.

faq.question: Can it handle sensitive files?
faq.answer: The skill can work with any files but will not access encrypted or password-protected files. Tell the skill to avoid specific sensitive folders.

faq.question: How do I maintain organization after cleanup?
faq.answer: The skill provides maintenance tips including weekly, monthly, and quarterly tasks to keep your files organized.
